rulings.Case Title: JO RICCOMI VS. UNIVERSITY CALIFORNIA HASTING COLLEGE OF THE LAW et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Peremptory Writ Of Mandamus
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, September 19, 2014, Line 9, PETITIONER JO RICCOMI's Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Mandamus The court grants respondent's request to augment the record pursuant to CCP 1094.5(e). The court denies the petition for writ of mandate. First, the 90-day decision period of section 216.8 of the MOU is directory and respondent's failure to comply with that time limitation does not warrant mandamus relief. See Bettencourt v. City and County of San Francisco (2007) 146 Cal.App.4th 1090, 1102. Second, respondent had the authority to suspend petitioner and place her on probation pursuant to sections 212.2 and 213.2 of the MOU. Third, petitioner fails to show that the other highlighted procedural errors, which respondent took account of when it reduced the penalty against petitioner, were prejudicial or mandate mandamus relief. The court strikes petitioner's supplemental reply brief. That brief raises new issues that the moving points and authorities do not address and the court did not grant petitioner leave to file a supplemental brief. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLOTTE ANN MAASSEN VS. MICHELLE MELISKO M.D
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Charlotte Ann Maassen And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, September 19, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Charlotte Ann Maassen And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Conor Moore, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant Regents of the University of California's motion is granted. No opposition was filed and Plaintiff failed to engage or respond to Defendant's attempts to meet and confer to appropriately schedule Plaintiff's deposition. Defendant is entitled to depose Plaintiff to prepare its defense. Plaintiff shall appear for her deposition no later than October 3, 2014. Plaintiff is also sanctioned for misuse of the discovery process and for failing to engage in the meet and confer process. Plaintiff is sanctioned in the amount of $720, payable to Defendant no later than October 31, 2014. The Court will sign the proposed order as submitted, as detailed in this ruling. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sfjudgeprotem@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: FOODCARE INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION (FORMERLY VS. FOODCALC LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion To Require X-Complainants To Furnish Bond
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, September 19, 2014, Line 3, PLAINTIFF FOODCARE INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION's Amended Notice Of Motion To Require Cross-Complainants To Furnish Bond Denied. Cross-Defendant Ken Marshall has not met his burden of establishing that "there is no reasonable possibility that the prosecution of the cause of action alleged in the complaint against the moving party will benefit the corporation or its shareholders." (Cal. Corp. Code sec. 800(c).) The Court did not consider the Declaration of Kenneth Marshall filed in support of this motion because it was not executed in compliance with CCP sec. 2015.5, requiring that a declaration must be made under penalty of perjury under the laws of California. (See also Kulshrestha v. First Union Commercial Corp. (2004) 33 Cal.4th 601, 617-18.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: HENRY M BURGOYNE III VS. GOLDSTERINRIGHT, A CALIFORNIA PROFESSIONAL CORPOR et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, September 19, 2014, Line 5, DEFENDANTS CALEGARI & MORRIS, ACCOUNTANTS, A PROFESSIONAL and MONIQUE TIGER, C.P.A.'s MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT The Motion to Strike is denied. As to the causes of action for aiding and abetting breach of fiduciary duty and unjust enrichment, the motion is denied as moot in light of the ruling on the Demurrer. The Motion is denied as to the request for punitive damages as Plaintiff has alleged a prima facie case of fraud which, if proven, entitles Plaintiff to punitive damages. (Cal. Civ. Code § 3294(a).) rulings.Case Title: HENRY M BURGOYNE III VS. GOLDSTERINRIGHT, A CALIFORNIA PROFESSIONAL CORPOR et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, September 19, 2014, Line 5, DEFENDANTS CALEGARI & MORRIS, ACCOUNTANTS, A PROFESSIONAL and MONIQUE TIGER, C.P.A. DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT The Demurrer is sustained in part and overruled in part. Both aiding and abetting causes of action are sustained without leave to amend due to Plaintiff's non-opposition. As to the cause of action for fraudulent concealment, the Demurrer is overruled. Plaintiff sufficiently alleged facts supporting Plaintiff's belief that Defendants acted with an intent to defraud.
